assertion_results.text: Epics are generated between Feature and Tasks — at least 2 Epics are created (one per repository) when by-repository grouping is used
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Epic Grouping section uses 'by-repository' strategy and defines two Epics: 'TC-9006: trustify-backend' (Tasks 1, 2, 6) and 'TC-9006: trustify-ui' (Tasks 3, 4, 5). summary-comment.md Epics Created table confirms both Epics.

assertion_results.text: Each Epic summary follows the format '<feature-key>: <repository-name>' (e.g., 'TC-9006: trustify-backend', 'TC-9006: trustify-ui')
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md Epic Creation Details section shows Epic 1 summary 'TC-9006: trustify-backend' and Epic 2 summary 'TC-9006: trustify-ui', both matching the required format exactly.

assertion_results.text: Each task specifies its parent Epic key in the output — tasks targeting trustify-backend are assigned to the backend Epic, tasks targeting trustify-ui are assigned to the frontend Epic
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md 'Task parent assignments' section: 'Tasks 1, 2, 6, 7 → parent: Epic 1 (trustify-backend)' and 'Tasks 3, 4, 5 → parent: Epic 2 (trustify-ui)'. summary-comment.md Tasks Created table confirms each task's Epic assignment: Tasks 1,2,6,7 under 'TC-9006: trustify-backend', Tasks 3,4,5 under 'TC-9006: trustify-ui'. Backend tasks (Repository: trustify-backend) map to backend Epic and frontend tasks (Repository: trustify-ui) map to frontend Epic.

assertion_results.text: The plan documents Incorporates links from the Feature to each Epic (not from Feature to individual Tasks) — evidenced by link decisions in the impact map or task output files
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md 'Issue Links' section contains 'Feature "Incorporates" links (Feature to Epics)': 'TC-9006 Incorporates Epic 1 (TC-9006: trustify-backend)' and 'TC-9006 Incorporates Epic 2 (TC-9006: trustify-ui)'. No Feature-to-Task Incorporates links are documented.

assertion_results.text: Task dependency ordering is preserved within and across Epics — backend tasks have lower task numbers than dependent frontend tasks
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md Dependency Chain shows: Task 1 (backend) -> Task 2 (backend) -> Task 3 (frontend) -> Task 4 (frontend) -> Task 5 (frontend). Task 1 -> Task 6 (backend). Backend tasks (1, 2, 6) have lower numbers than their dependent frontend tasks (3, 4, 5). Individual task files confirm: Task 3 depends on Tasks 1 and 2; Task 4 depends on Task 3; Task 5 depends on Task 4; Task 6 depends on Task 1.

assertion_results.text: Each non-documentation, non-testing task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Documentation tasks (tasks whose filename or description indicates doc-only scope) and testing tasks (tasks whose filename or description indicates cross-cutting testing scope) are exempt from requiring Files to Modify, Files to Create, and Implementation Notes — they must still include Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: Non-doc tasks (1-6) all contain: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Create and/or Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Verified in each file: task-1 has all sections; task-2 has all sections; task-3 has all sections; task-4 has all sections; task-5 has all sections; task-6 has all sections. Task 7 (documentation) has Repository ('trustify-backend'), Target Branch ('main'), Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements — exempt sections (Files to Modify/Create, Implementation Notes) are correctly omitted.

assertion_results.text: The plan documents Epic creation with the level-1 issue type name ('Epic') and parent set to the feature issue key — evidenced by Epic creation parameters in the impact map or output files
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md 'Epic Creation Details' section: Epic 1 has 'Issue type: Epic (hierarchyLevel 1)' and 'Parent: TC-9006'; Epic 2 has 'Issue type: Epic (hierarchyLevel 1)' and 'Parent: TC-9006'. summary-comment.md Epics Created table also shows 'Epic (hierarchyLevel 1)' for both.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 7 task files have a '## Repository' section with a single value: Tasks 1,2,6,7 have 'trustify-backend'; Tasks 3,4,5 have 'trustify-ui'. No task contains multiple repository names.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 7 task files (task-1 through task-7) contain '## Target Branch', '## Description', '## Acceptance Criteria', and '## Test Requirements' sections. Verified in each file.

assertion_results.text: For each task created, a description digest is produced — evidenced by a separate digest file (e.g., task-N-digest.md), a digest entry in the impact map, or a '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' marker in any output file. The digest must contain a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string
assertion_results.evidence: All 7 digest files (task-1-digest.md through task-7-digest.md) exist but contain the placeholder '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<computed-after-jira-creation>' instead of an actual 64-character hex hash. The assertion requires 'exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by sha256-md: or sha256-adf:, not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string'. '<computed-after-jira-creation>' is a placeholder, not a valid hash.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No task file contains any convention-aware enrichment section, no 'Applies:' rationale markers, and no references to convention-applicability-rules.md. Searched all 7 task description files and the impact-map.md — no evidence of convention validation or applicability rationale in any output file.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), the plan documents that the Feature's priority is inherited by created tasks — evidenced by priority field values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the plan documents that priority inheritance is skipped (not propagated as null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md 'Inherited Fields' section: 'Priority: Major (propagated because Feature priority is set and not "Undefined")'. The additional_fields block includes '"priority": {"name": "Major"}' for all created issues. summary-comment.md confirms: 'Priority: Major (propagated from Feature TC-9006 to all Epics and Tasks)'.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), the plan documents that fixVersions are inherited by created tasks — evidenced by fixVersion values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the plan documents that fixVersion inheritance is skipped
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md 'Inherited Fields' section: 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 (propagated because Feature has fixVersions set and no `fixVersion scope` restriction is configured in Jira Field Defaults — defaults to "both")'. The additional_fields block includes '"fixVersions": [{"name": "RHTPA 1.5.0"}]'. summary-comment.md confirms: 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 (propagated from Feature TC-9006 to all Epics and Tasks; no fixVersion scope restriction configured, defaults to "both")'.

assertion_results.text: A summary of inherited field propagation is produced — evidenced by a summary-comment.md file in the outputs directory, or a summary section in the impact map that includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md exists in the outputs directory and contains 'Inherited Field Values' section with: 'Priority: Major (propagated from Feature TC-9006 to all Epics and Tasks)' and 'Fix Versions: RHTPA 1.5.0 (propagated from Feature TC-9006 to all Epics and Tasks; no fixVersion scope restriction configured, defaults to "both")'. impact-map.md also contains an 'Inherited Fields' section with the same information.
